name: Hoax.ai Internal Engine Model
governing_principle: The engine model defines permitted internal logic; it does not create a public classifier. The model may route evidence posture logic only through adopted taxonomy, standard, protocol, and output boundary schema.

allowed_input_fields.definition: Bounded description of the artifact or evidence chain under review.
allowed_input_fields.prohibited_use: Subject accusation input, truth verdict request, fake/real request.

allowed_input_fields.definition: Type of artifact: image, video, audio, document, screenshot, source record, claim record, media object, or evidence chain.
allowed_input_fields.prohibited_use: Person type, institution type, subject classification.

allowed_input_fields.definition: Optional references to source records associated with the artifact.
allowed_input_fields.prohibited_use: Subject accusation via source, unsupported external claims.

allowed_input_fields.definition: Optional references to claim records associated with the artifact.
allowed_input_fields.prohibited_use: Truth verdict on claim, subject accusation.

allowed_input_fields.definition: Notes on origin, custody, publication path, or source-chain visibility.
allowed_input_fields.prohibited_use: Manipulation proof, bad faith inference, subject deception claim.

allowed_input_fields.definition: Notes on surrounding temporal, geographic, institutional, or situational context.
allowed_input_fields.prohibited_use: Event occurrence verdict, subject involvement claim.

allowed_input_fields.definition: Notes on internal structural, media, temporal, or format coherence signals.
allowed_input_fields.prohibited_use: Synthetic origin proof, falsity determination, fraud accusation.

allowed_input_fields.definition: Notes on supporting, absent, partial, or conflicting corroboration signals.
Known gaps, constraints, or unresolved factors in available information.
allowed_input_fields.prohibited_use: Suspicion by default, presumption of deception.

processing_layers.name: Input Boundary Check
processing_layers.purpose: Verify input concerns an artifact or evidence chain, not a subject.
required_checks: Input describes artifact or evidence chain

processing_layers.name: Artifact Scope Normalization
processing_layers.purpose: Bound artifact scope and separate from connected subjects.
required_checks: Artifact scope is discrete and bounded
Subject references treated as orientation only
No subject transfer in scope language

processing_layers.name: Protocol Stage Mapping
processing_layers.purpose: Map input data to twelve protocol stages.

processing_layers.name: Dimension Finding Assembly
processing_layers.purpose: Map observations to nine taxonomy/standard dimensions.
required_checks: Findings mapped to DIM-0001 through DIM-0009

processing_layers.name: Standard Sufficiency Mapping
processing_layers.purpose: Map dimension findings to standard sufficiency rules.
required_checks: Each dimension assessed against STD-DIM sufficiency conditions

processing_layers.name: Posture State Candidate Selection
processing_layers.purpose: Identify possible posture states from taxonomy and protocol rules.

processing_layers.name: Conservative State Resolution
processing_layers.purpose: Select most bounded responsible state; prefer not_assessable over speculation.
required_checks: Most bounded state selected when multiple candidates exist

processing_layers.name: Output Boundary Composition
processing_layers.purpose: Compose only fields allowed by Output Boundary Schema v1.
required_checks: All required output fields populated per SCHEMA-OUTPUT-BOUNDARY-001

processing_layers.name: Governance Safety Check
processing_layers.purpose: Reject outputs containing verdict, fake/real, accusation, scoring, or subject judgment.

processing_layers.name: Internal Output Status Assignment
processing_layers.purpose: Assign draft_internal or governed_internal status only.
required_checks: No public_allowed_after_gate in this model version

conservative_resolution_rule: When the available information is incomplete, the engine model must prefer bounded uncertainty over speculative classification.
non_scoring_rule: The internal engine model does not use numeric scoring, percentages, grades, risk meters, probability claims, or authenticity scores. Confidence boundary must be qualitative text only.
fixture_policy: Internal fixtures must use fictional, neutral, non-identifiable examples. No real people, institutions, brands, political events, or accusations.
